rulings.Case Title: LENA A. AJAMIAN VS. CANTORCO2E, L.P., A PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Otion For Orde Rquashing Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 30, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF LENA AJAMIAN'S (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Subpoenas Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion, subject to court approval of the terms of an appropriate protective order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interoggatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 30, 2012, Line 4. Cross Defendant Todd Theodora's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interoggatories Set One. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Granted in part. Mr. Chahine shall provide further responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 stating the facts upon which he bases his denial of Requests for Admissions Nos. 13, 14, 15 and 20. Request for attorneys fees and costs denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 30, 2012, Line 4. Cross Defendant Todd Theodora's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Granted in part. Mr. Chahine shall produce invoices for which he seeks compensation from the Theodora Oringher firm as an element of his damages. The hours spent and dollar amounts charged shall not be redacted, but attorney-client privileged communications can otherwise be redacted. Mr. Chahine shall also produce any correspondence with opposing parties, discovery requests, or response to discovery requests from the other actions for which he seeks compensation. Request for attorneys fees and costs denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Special Interoggatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 30, 2012, Line 4. Cross Defendant Todd Theodora's Motion To Special Interoggatories Set One. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Granted. The incorporation of other interrogatory responses by reference without summarizing or quoting from the information does not meet the requirements of Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal. App. 3d 771, 783. Moreover, the answers that incorporate a reference to documents do not comply with C.C.P. Section 2030.230 because, according to Cross-Complainant's Chahine's own responses, a compilation, abstract, audit, or summary of the records already exists in the referenced documents. Request for attorneys fees and costs denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 30, 2012, Line 4. Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions Set One. The court adopts in full the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: DENIED. Cross-Complainant Chahine has unequivocally denied each and every one of Cross-Defendant Theodora Oringher's requests for admission, and therefore the denials comply with the requirements of C.C.P. 2033.220. Request for attorneys fees and costs denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
